_meta.purpose: Compare v6 initial run vs v6 rerender experiment to identify which actions are reliable signal vs noise. This comparison informed v6b action space fixes.

naming_clarification.v6_initial: First 10-obj run with v6 codebase (evolution_v6_full). Several flat_no_rim objects show delta=0.0 for L_RIM_UP, possibly due to rendering pipeline not applying the action correctly in that run.
naming_clarification.v6_rerender: Second 10-obj run (evolution_v6_rerender) with fresh Blender renders per action attempt. Produced non-zero deltas and is considered the more reliable measurement. Data from this run directly informed v6b Fix A and Fix B.
naming_clarification.important: v6_initial delta=0.0 for L_RIM_UP on obj_004/006/008 is suspicious and may indicate a rendering application bug, not a true neutral result. v6_rerender corrects this.

v6_initial.note: Both actions failed in initial run. May be VLM noise or render not applied.

v6_rerender.note: L_WORLD_EV_UP succeeded. This directly motivated v6b Fix B: swap order to try L_WORLD_EV_UP first.

v6b_full_result.note: With Fix B (L_WORLD_EV_UP first), delta improved further (+0.027). VLM variance partially explains difference from rerender (+0.012).

obj_002.conclusion: underexposed_global is solvable. L_WORLD_EV_UP is the effective action. Ordering matters.

attempt_0.note: Suspicious zero — likely rendering not applied

v6_rerender.note: Both L_RIM_UP and compound L_RIM_FILL_UP negative. Confirms Fix A needed.

v6b_full_result.note: v6b Fix A replacement actions also failed. flat_no_rim is not solvable with current lighting action space.

obj_004.conclusion: flat_no_rim on this object: no lighting action effective. Root cause likely mesh geometry.

obj_006.conclusion: flat_no_rim: marginally negative, below eps. No gain.

attempt_0.note: Positive! But may be VLM noise rather than real rendering improvement.

v6_initial.note: obj_007 was the only flat_no_rim object that appeared to improve with L_RIM_UP in the initial run. This was inconsistent with all other flat_no_rim objects and inconsistent with the rerender experiment.

v6_rerender.note: Rerender experiment showed +0.0057 but still below eps. Not reliable enough to accept.

obj_007.conclusion: obj_007 L_RIM_UP appears to give small positive delta (+0.006-0.011) but it is near the VLM noise floor. Not reliable enough to exceed eps=0.01. Counts as neutral/borderline, not as proof L_RIM_UP is useful.

obj_008.conclusion: flat_no_rim: both L_RIM_UP and replacement actions negative. Consistent across all measurements.

flat_no_rim_summary.pattern: 3/4 negative, 1/4 small positive but below eps=0.01. Net: L_RIM_UP is not a reliable fix for flat_no_rim.

v6b_fix_a_result.finding: Replacement actions also fail to improve flat_no_rim objects. The issue is not L_RIM_UP specifically — the flat appearance is not solvable via Blender lighting parameters with current mesh geometry.

v6b_motivating_insight.Fix_A: L_RIM_UP harmful for 3/4 flat_no_rim objects in rerender. Replace with less risky alternatives.
v6b_motivating_insight.Fix_B: L_WORLD_EV_UP effective for underexposed_global in rerender (+0.012). Move to first position.
v6b_motivating_insight.Fix_C: Align flat_lighting fallback order with Fix A to avoid L_RIM_UP being tried via issue_tags path.
